name: Florida Keys Bread Pudding with Rum Sauce
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Sponge cake, day old, cut into cubes
1 can Pineapple, crushed, well drained (8 oz)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Combine the coconut with the milk in a food processor and process until fairly smooth. Strain the milk into a bowl. Reserve the coconut for use at another time.
Combine 1 3/4 cups of the coconut milk with the cream in a medium saucepan. Heat over low heat until hot, about 5 minutes.
Beat the egg yolks with sugar in a large bowl until very light and fluffy. Slowly whisk in the hot coconut milk mixture. Transfer to the top of a double boiler. Cook over simmering water, stirring constantly, for 5 minutes. Stir in the rum.
Place half the bread cubes on the bottom of a 1 1/2 to 2 quart souffle dish. Spread the pineapple evenly over the top. Cover the pineapple with the remaining bread cubes. Pour the hot egg mixture evenly over the top and let stand for 10 minutes.
Bake the pudding until set, about 40 minutes. Cool on a wire rack. Serve slightly warm or at room temperature with Rum Sauce. SERVES: 6-8
RUM SAUCE:
Combine the cream and milk in a medium saucepan. Heat over low heat until hot, about 5 minutes.
Beat the egg yolks with the sugar in a large bowl until light. Slowly whisk in the hot liquid. Whisk in the rum. Transfer to the top of a double boiler. Cook over simmering water, stirring constantly, for 10 minutes or until thick enough to coat a spoon. Cool to room temperature. Serve at room temperature or slightly chilled. MAKES: about 1 1/2 cups
